content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reengineered to tier curiosity signals by how reliably an agent remembers prior surprising exploratory encounters: memories of unresolved surprising transitions are prioritized for active re-sampling, while well-memorized low-prediction-error curiosity targets are deprioritized. This creates a self-reinforcing exploration loop that avoids the common stagnation traps of both fixed intrinsic reward methods and unguided active learning.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
